questions.question: Explain the difference between '=' and '==' operators in Java with examples.
keyPoints: '=' is assignment operator that assigns value to variable
'==' is comparison operator that checks equality
Example: int x = 5; assigns 5 to x; if(x == 5) compares if x equals 5

questions.question: What are compound assignment operators? List any four with examples.
keyPoints: Compound operators combine operation with assignment
Example: a += 5 is same as a = a + 5

questions.question: Explain chained assignment in Java with an example.
keyPoints: Chained assignment assigns same value to multiple variables
Right to left evaluation: c gets value, then b, then a

questions.question: What is the difference between primitive and reference assignment in Java?
keyPoints: Reference assignment means both variables point to same object

questions.question: Evaluate: int a = 5; a += a++ + ++a; Show the step-by-step execution.
What will be the output of: int x = 10; x -= 5 + 2; System.out.println(x); Explain.
Write a Java program demonstrating the use of various assignment operators (simple and compound) with integer variables. Show output for each operation.
keyPoints: Show before and after values for each operation

questions.question: Explain the concept of object reference assignment in Java. Create an example showing how two reference variables point to the same object and modification through one affects the other.
keyPoints: Explain reference variables store memory addresses
Show both references reflect the change
